data.body:
[G4 child-consistency — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with THIS child (S6 1ba5: Docs — private-repo credentials, descriptive errors, ref/source semantics, HMAC trust model)]

Ran the 7 cross-child interaction modes for this docs child against the WHOLE sibling roster (S1 6d56, S2 70c7, S3 fa49, S4 30b3, S5 b7fa). All 7 modes are clean under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. EMPTY G4 findings list for this pairing.

Mode-by-mode:
1. Implicit shared state — CLEAN. Every value this child documents has a single named producer: REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR + the portability seam (S1), disk-cap/janitor behavior (S2), the descriptive credential error message+remedy (S1 AC5 raises it), ref/source + the origin/main default (S3 threads, S5 surfaces), the in-toto Statement shape + HMAC limits (S4, whose What explicitly delegates the trust-model docs "DOCUMENTED in S6"). Documented values are mutually consistent; no shared-state value is documented without a producer.

2. Conflicting assumptions — CLEAN. Every doc claim matches the behavior it describes: attested = pinned SHA / signs / records verified_at_sha (S4 AC1, S3 AC1/AC4); local = in-place checkout / never signs (S3 AC4 marks-unsigned, S4 AC3 bars-signing); "attested fails closed while local still runs" (S1 AC5); origin/main default (S3 AC2 / S5 AC1). No contradiction with any sibling.

3. Dependency gap — CLEAN. This child is a pure documenter; each documented obligation is claimed by a named sibling producer (credential error S1; ref/source S3+S5; in-toto/HMAC S4; tmpdir/disk-cap S1/S2). The standing S3<->S4 attest.py snapshot-handle routing seam is a code-side dependency gap that does not touch this docs child and is out of this pairing's scope. Verified the named doc surfaces are real: docs/jira-sync-setup.md exists; README.md exists and already carries REBAR_ROOT in the MCP-setup block (README.md:193,:261); REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR is a new (pre-manifest) env not yet in the tree — consistent with S1 owning its introduction.

4. Scope overlap — CLEAN. ref/source: this child owns the human PROSE; S5 owns the inline schema-text/documented-defaults — each scoped out of the other (S5 Scope: "Out: ... prose docs (S6)"; this child Scope: "Out: code (S1-S5)"). in-toto Statement: S4 BUILDS the envelope, this child DOCUMENTS it (S4 What names the S6 hand-off). Clean producer/documenter splits; no double-implementation of one artifact.

5. Ordering violation — CLEAN. NOTE/correction to the roster's prior reviews: the children do NOT all carry empty deps — the actual graph is S1(6d56)->{S2 70c7, S3 fa49}, S3->{S4 30b3, S5 b7fa}, and BOTH S4 and S5 -> blocks THIS docs child (30b3 and b7fa each carry a `blocks 1ba5` edge). For this pairing that encoded ordering is COHERENT with the producer->documenter flow (behaviors S4/S5 precede the docs that describe them); it asserts no contradiction or gap against this child. So no ordering-violation finding here — the dependency edges, where present, point the benign way.

6. Consumer impact — CLEAN. This child is a terminal consumer/documenter; it produces no API/schema/env surface that a sibling depends on, so it imposes no downstream breakage.

7. Residual references — CLEAN. All cross-refs resolve: docs/jira-sync-setup.md (exists), README.md (exists), REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR (S1), disk-cap (S2), the EFS/NFS flock caveat xref alto-fruit-punch (matches the parent's Out-of-scope note), the in-toto Statement (S4), and "Out: code (S1-S5)" (all five siblings present in the roster).

Note (not a G4 finding on THIS pairing): the standing dependency-gap on sibling S4 (30b3) about routing the pinned-SHA snapshot handle into plan_review/attest.py's dependency_hashes()/_rehash()/claim-gate freshness re-check is a code seam between S3 and S4; it does not touch this docs child. S6 documents the HMAC trust model (delegated from S4) regardless of how that seam is resolved and introduces no new inconsistency.

Conclusion: no high-confidence cross-child inconsistency between this docs child and the parent/siblings. Empty G4 findings list for this pairing.
